All right, so now we are getting into the next part of retention, which is member events and workshops Okay, so let's dive right into the key factors that we are going to be going over in this lesson Always host a monthly event. Okay, so consistency is king Don't don't forget to stop hosting them just because you had a really cool one. You want to keep those going every 30 days or so Events do not need to be expensive Just get everyone together That's what makes an amazing event high attendance a lot of fun not, you know, how pricey the event is Get your entire staff involved. So not just you as the owner Find a way make a way to get everyone on your team involved in the events every time you have one Use your gym for workshops and events So we'll mostly be covering events in this lesson, but same thing with workshops workshops are very similar to events They just have a learning theme around them where you're teaching your members something So use your gym for that you don't need to rent out big spaces or anything like that Maybe you just need to get a little bit creative with decorating your gym or setting it up correctly Plan events well into the future so people can save the date This is key a lot of times people would love to come to your events, but they just already have plans So you need to make sure that you get your dates out there early enough to where you are their plans All right, and then announce announce announce, okay? Make sure you're always announcing your events. So you have high attendance. That is going to be super important So this is everything that we're going to cover this will will work for your Your large group members your one-on-one or HTTP HTTP clients or online clients. However, however, you want to get people together. This will work for all of them So let's start off with the event schedule. All right You want to run events or workshops every month. All right, so always be promoting an event You always want to be promoting something if there's a time where you're not really promoting an event You probably should be as soon as one event ends That's when you want to start promoting the next event So like let's say you have a an event on a Saturday that next Monday You want to get new flyers up a new post on Facebook and start talking about the next event because people are going to want to You know take advantage of it So you'll post all the pictures from your previous event Maybe I like that Sunday like let's say you have an event on Saturday Sunday You should be posting all the pictures of what happened on social media all that kind of stuff Show people how amazing it was show them how strong your culture is and then immediately start advertising the next event on Monday This shows your members consistency and keeps momentum and engagement in the gym high Okay, and then it really builds the culture and community when you're always doing something as a team So this is just an example of an event calendar with the dates circled that you have events we'll get even more detailed as to what is going to be happening on these events and When people can sign up and how they can sign up but at a bare minimum You should have the dates reserved for your entire team and for your members to be able to see All right spend time not money Some of the best events are ones that are planned out really well in thought of beforehand Okay, so a really well planned event like a like a five mile hike could be much more impactful Then a and fun then a like poorly planned Get together at a park that includes like park fees catering a bounce house Like you could you could load in all this stuff and it doesn't mean it's gonna have a bigger impact or Better result than one that's completely free. So planning is paramount. Okay Expensive does not equal successful. It doesn't matter how much you spend it could be totally unsuccessful If something is poorly planned it could have the adverse effect On your culture or Jim then you're looking for like if something is just really poorly planned and it's and people are complaining and there There's no parking. There's no whatever it may be It could be a deterrent it could hurt your retention because people like man These guys just don't have their stuff together So you need to take time to think of every scenario that could happen at an event and then plan for those contingencies Okay, so that's all part of the planning beforehand spend time So we're gonna go over some examples of some free events That are a lot of fun if planned correctly. So the first one that we're gonna go over is Hikes, I think I'm gonna give you like five or six of them Which is like a half a year's worth of events that you could do that are basically completely free So like a hike we've done this before and it's been really fun. So plan the trail Do the hike beforehand like do it yourself just so you so you really know Because there's reviews and all that stuff but what if you do it and there's just a huge mud pit somewhere in the middle and It ruins it right plan for weather Agree on a start time. So everybody knows when you're kicking off Tell everybody how long it should take. So sometimes when you say like five miles Somebody might not really know how long that's gonna take or maybe have it estimated correctly And they might have other plans and the last thing you want is for them to be like, hey I have to be done and they're just halfway through the hike. It's like, well, there's no other faster way to get back So make sure you let them know how long it should take based on you doing it Let them know what they should bring a lot of people would have no idea what to bring and they need to pack accordingly Plan for transportation or parking a lot of times when you go for a hike it depends on the on the path But there's not very good parking or at least not enough for an entire gym's worth So make sure you plan for that and pick a correct spot based on that and then bring a first aid kit So there's like bugs and animals and someone could roll a ankle on a rock or something like that as a gym owner you should have a First aid kit in your pack just in case something were to happen But other than that it could be super fun and completely free and it's just it's awesome. It's a it's a healthy Exercise activity that people will absolutely love and it's just a lot of fun. So that's something that you could do Whether permitting that would be a huge success if you plan it another one could be a themed boot camp So these are a lot of fun, too, and it utilizes your current setup So all you're doing is changing one or two of your classes like we'll have two 45-minute classes back-to-back and instead of that we might just have one Our long class because people come in late or they'll bring a friend who doesn't really know that you have to be there extra Early or whatever it may be so we'll kill the classes those two Saturdays and we'll we'll have one big longer class And it's totally themed it could be like an 80s theme. It could be like a your superhero theme It could be whatever you want and and people love to dress up and work out and have fun So pick a theme decorate the gym, you know put some put some stuff all over the place to make it look really cool Get the entire staff to show up, which we'll talk about how we're gonna do that Ask everyone to bring a healthy dish people love bringing stuff You'll only get like 25% of the people to bring it, but they'll bring enough to where the entire the entire event Members and everybody there there's gonna be plenty for them. So it's still completely free for you Make it normal than a different workout. So like have like fitness games or just make it fun So like maybe instead of flipping tires They have to shoot a medicine ball into a tire and keep doing push-ups until they make it in or something You know have have fun with it and make it make it fun themed and include some stuff that you don't normally do in your workouts Again always allow them to bring a friend. These are the best kind of workouts Like I don't like saying hey your friends workouts always free at my gym, you know, whenever they want to drop in I don't mind them bringing a friend, but I want it to be when I'm showcasing my best stuff. So this would be a perfect a Perfect opportunity for them to bring a friend and maybe even have a way for them to get signed up after the event Hey, did you like it? I'll fill out this card or you know, let's book an appointment or let's you know However, you want to do it to make sure that you have a follow-up conversation with that friend, but always allow it Allow it to be interactive, right? So maybe they get to coach the coaches for a little bit maybe have like a five-minute segment where it's like alright guys Now you guys get to coach us. So if all of your coaches are there That could be a ton of fun. So they're making you do burpees or making you do whatever that they absolutely hate doing that's a great way for them to be able to you know, give you guys a little bit some back and You know create some laughs and create a good bond Maybe they'll they'll some people will film it of the coaches having to do this stuff that they are always making other people do and it Can just it allows for it to be a lot of fun and interactive And then and like I said base the whole theme around fun base It doesn't have to be the hardest workout in the world. That's just driving them to the ground. It's about building your culture That's what this is for you can do hard workouts the other six days of the week but this is around, you know having fun and showing them that you guys can let loose and and fitness it can be serious but It can also be a ton of fun. That's what this kind of event is for another way. It's free. So The next one I want to go over is 5k runs. These are a lot of fun and again, they're they're free, right? So Typically when you're doing a 5k run and these are 5k runs that are set up by the gym not like an event sponsored one where Everybody has to pay like $25 or $30. Those are fine, too If you're in a pinch and you don't have time to like set things up or to plan anything or plot a course or anything like that Those are those are cool, but if you want to keep it completely free. This is the best way to do it so first thing you do is you pick a cause everybody loves to do it for a cause and it gets them to You know dress up or or support that cause like we always do one for breast cancer awareness so we we dress up in pink and Everybody comes and they donate like we don't have a minimum donation So you can donate whatever you want. You don't have to donate anything if you don't want to But we always donate that to a place called play for pink after the event So plot the course after you have picked the cause that you want to get people involved in plot the course So plot a 5k, you know 3.2 miles that starts and ends at your gym That's the easiest way to do it for transportation and all things considered have it start and then go around the neighborhood or around wherever wherever your gym is and have you know have it turn around and get back to your gym within 5k So by doing that you'll need to put out cones with directions Otherwise people might get lost unless it's a super easy course So I always ran them first and then put cones with like arrows taped to the cones I'm like where to turn and where to go as soon as like it's mostly just for the first couple people because then everybody else is Just following them but do that in advance. I learned the hard way Obtained permission from the city. So that depends on where you're at But like as an example for us the city has no problem with us doing it They just have to we just have to obtain permission and request it like 30 days in advance So that might be something you have to do as well depending on your city Print out maps so give each print like print out like 40 maps just like we did on this picture here You just do it on map quest or something and print them out in case somebody wants to review it before they're gonna take off We I'm only saying that because we had a lot of people request them Then have water at the halfway point so put a little table or a chair or something with a water jug and some cups and a A garbage bag at the halfway point let them know it's the halfway point so they can take a little break I put it on the map You can kind of see here that I put a little halfway point on the map with the water Symbol so they knew that they could get water there, but it's great because some people are really tired at that point Use your coaches as Pacers So make sure that if your coaches are going to be there Which they should be that they are pacing everybody and not just bolting off to the front and leaving everybody in the dust Like we already know they're in awesome shape like they should be helping everybody And they should you should always have a coach at the end like at the rear to make sure that nobody gets left behind Do not leave anybody behind in a 5k. That is the worst So make sure that they stay at the back and they're motivating somebody even if people are just walking the whole thing Which you totally will have people walking the whole thing But make sure you have a coach with them every step of the way So and then have a fun area for the kids so some kids that people will bring their kids We always had like maybe a couple of our managers or admin staff or whoever just stay back Because you want people there you want people at the gym when they start getting back, right? And you can do little metals we like to give out metals that doesn't keep it free But metals are like two bucks three bucks a piece you can totally do that But if you want to keep it free not necessary anyways Make sure that you have a couple of staff members at the gym from people start rolling back And they can also watch the kids if the kids are doing some kind of like fun activity like Frisbee or Playing with like basketballs or something like that just have an area for the kids to just it's not that long of a time period Most people will be done within the hour or so Make sure you have something for the kids to do during that time So that is another really fun event that you can run weather permitting that will cost you guys nothing But it has a big return The next thing that the next one that we're gonna cover would be going over parents and kids events so something that purposely involves the parents with the kids as an example here We just did like a mommy and me yoga and it was a blast, right a bunch of moms got to bring their their kids and go through yoga poses and hold it and all and all that kind of stuff and Essentially like show their kids and be a healthy example for them So what you'll want to do to pull this one off is pick a workout Make sure it's kids safe like don't be doing you know mommy and me Olympic lifts Do something that is not so aggressive and it's super safe for kids to do Explain it to parents and the flyer or it won't work They won't they won't if you don't explain like what it is and what their kids are gonna be doing They're just too protective and just won't bring them. So make sure that you are super clear about what the workout is going to entail And then make sure you have some kids safe fun music So you're not bumping, you know some kind of stuff that parents would be uncomfortable with their kids listening to So make sure you have good music for that and lots of fun short activities like kids can't go through an hour-long Yoga class like they'll get they'll get bored and distracted like within five ten minutes. So you'll need to Have like maybe you know five or six different activities planned that involve yoga or involve whatever your theme is But they're broken up with plenty of breaks in between and like totally shifts direction on the kids mindset So then they have fun all over again like every five six minutes And that's just the nature of keeping them engaged when it comes to things like fitness So the more fun you can have Or the more fun the kids can have the more successful your event is gonna be like the parents are just doing it for their kids Parents and kids work together. So have them work together On a common like exercise So they're helping each other out that bonding is really really important And this is a cool way for them to be able to do that and then intertwine your Gym so the the parents and the kids will have good memories together And if it's at your gym, that's even better. That's even stronger and stickier and it's great for retention And then make sure you have snacks So maybe like a bag of carrots and some whatever snacks fruits and vegetables work great for parents But make sure that you have snacks at your gym because kids They'll they'll typically Want those a lot of times parents parents bring their own snacks But it's good for you to be able to offer that and bring that for them and it makes it seem like more of an event So any kind of parent kids events, you know yoga boot camp kickboxing all that stuff just have fun with it Do balance training all sorts of stuff But that's a great way to have a fun event and make it completely free The next one we'll go over is fundraiser boot camps. So these are a lot of fun Essentially what you're doing is you're raising you're raising funds or prizes for a great cause So you're asking for donations So you want to set up the workout first the big boot camp is fine Whatever your normal style workout that you're really comfortable running is completely fine You could do it at your gym outside of your gym But the whole point is for that month you are collecting Donations whether it's monetary or it is just prizes or toys or food or whatever it may be And then you're issuing out tickets To win prizes and all the prizes are going to be donated from local businesses So we've done this five years in a row and super successful like businesses love giving out gift baskets If it's for a great cause so you're gonna put your logo or I'm sorry You're gonna put the businesses logo at your gym on the gift basket with what's inside of it. You're going to And they usually always give out like some kind of free service of theirs Which in turn is why they're doing it because all of a sudden there's gonna be a lot of people test driving their services that maybe didn't before Try to find businesses that are complimentary to your business like a massage place. Maybe um, a nutritionist maybe Anything local that would make a lot of sense not like a try to like avoid You know businesses that don't align with what your your values are And you'll get a lot of businesses like I would say 50 of everybody you approach has no problem Giving you a gift basket of like stuff that doesn't cost them a lot These are what people can win at the end of your boot camp. So at the end of your boot camp you do a raffle ticket drawing And they got all these raffle tickets from everything that they donated And they can win these really cool prizes. I've done this a lot and it's a ton of fun Super successful as soon as you get the gift baskets from the vendors from the local businesses You start putting them up on the tables and so everybody can see what they're winning with a with a description of what's inside of it Next they can see what they're winning and people get so motivated. They keep on bringing more toys They keep making five dollar donations and at the end you take all those toys and all those donations And you go you go give it to the the cause that you're donating for so focus on fun This really builds community because it's all local community. I would highly recommend Having the cause that you're donating to be something local versus something national or worldwide You'll get way more participation if you're like raising funds for like a local elementary school or something like that Where all the parents are involved in it's part of their community You'll get way more participation than if you're doing like a huge national thing So this is a great event to do. I highly recommend it. It's a lot of fun And you'll you'll draw a lot of people in attention with this one completely free if you do it right You just have to be the one that orchestrates it all the the businesses will donate the gifts The the members will bring in all the money and the prizes You just have to run the workouts and and have a place that orchestrates it the um The next piece that will go over so those those are the events that I wanted to cover that are completely free I'm in a lot of fun. There's a lot more, but I'm just giving you some examples Um, so the next couple things that we want to cover is getting the whole team involved Okay, so not just you as the owner, but everybody so here's how here's the best way to do it Tell your entire team the coaches and the admin that events are mandatory unless they have like pre approved time off But just tell them right off the bat like hey part of part of working here is you attend all the member events Right. So the same the same exact schedule That you're posting in your gym putting on your wall give it to your staff so you can say there's so there's no excuses Like hey, here's all the dates that you'll need to reserve or plan accordingly for Um, and then finally, uh, they will have fun like at first they may be like all these events They're gonna have fun when they're in a big group and it's a fun focused event And they're with all their members like they're gonna have a good time. They always do So this will strengthen your team and your member culture Not just your member culture, but your team culture as well because you guys are all having fun together Especially when you start doing things like mud races and all that kind of stuff. It's a blast So get your whole team involved whether they are for it or not for it It's just part of their job And they'll have a lot of fun and your members will certainly appreciate it and they'll want to come Knowing that all of their trainers are there as well Next part use your gym. Okay. So your gym can be used to host events that you normally, uh, you'd have to rent out a hall for You have a gym you have a gym with good amount of square footage Just put some time into making it look good, you know decorated, you know get it all, uh Layed out make it look really cool and themed and everything like that And what I would recommend doing is alternating between Events that are at the gym and outside of the gym So you don't have every single event outside of the gym or every single event at the gym But alternate between the two and it just creates a lot of diversity and fun So planned a theme boot camps events on saturday morning when attendance is normally high And if you do that if all of your themed boot camps are on like saturdays when it's super busy You're gonna have a built-in high like show rate because people are just gonna come for their regular workout or they're gonna be like Yeah, I can totally do that event because it's it's when they're working out anyways And that's a great way to get momentum kicked off. So if you don't if you're like, hey, man, I don't do events right now Um, no problem. Uh, this is a great way to get them kicked off is use your built-in busy times and just theme it And then all of a sudden there'll be a lot of momentum around your events And the next one you could do it at not such a popular time or outside of the gym And you'll still have really good attendance because you have pictures and content and people talking about The last event that they went to which just so happened to be during your busy boot camp time Next plan the year. So we kind of talked about this but uh, Plan the year in advance. Uh, this way People can reserve the time so post it up at the gym and in the facebook group So give all the details uh and sign-up sheets make sure uh, there's an event board at your gym and it's visible and it's super fun So like whether it's a big chalkboard or whether it is something that you custom make or whatever it may be Just make sure that you have it up at the gym and you are uh 100 percent, uh For sure on the dates and the times with as many details as you can provide This way people can save the dates and they can see everything the whole year in advance and pick which ones they want to go to It just makes it a ton of fun and it shows that you guys do think ahead and that Your events and your culture is really important to you. It's not like a spur of the moment thing Um, and then finally announce So announcing your events is super important. Okay. High attendance is what makes them fun and builds the culture Uh, which increases retention the stronger your culture the stronger your retention is so having a lot of people actually do these events Is is the whole point right? There's no point in doing all and putting forth all this effort into events if people aren't coming to it So in order to get them there you need to make the announcements Uh, they come they enjoy it. They feel like they're part of something but it all starts with you guys announcing it So here's uh, when you should be announcing. Okay, first thing is flyers at the gym and on social media So pay somebody on five or like five ten dollars to create these amazing Flyers for you, which is super easy to do you just give them an idea of what you're thinking and they'll they'll come up with them And then and then print them off and post them all over your gym for that month's events And then use it to post on facebook as well in the groups and and always promote your events that way just visually second, um before and after classes your coaches should be um making announcements before and after classes for each one of the events Um, and that way people if they don't pay attention to where they're walking or they're not on facebook a lot The trainers are actually telling them. All right Um the reach out text so by now or after implementing these five horsemen of retention You should be sending text messages out to your um members on a 14 day cadence Maybe even more frequently depending on how you run it Um, but this is a great place to put in those reach out reminders Or a great a great place to put in the event reminders is into these reach outs So you could say hey Just wanted to make sure that everything's good going with nutrition and fitness and accountability And is there anything you need for me? You definitely want your challengers coming to events Because it really showcases how strong your community is and will impact their decision to Continue or not. So there's really no downside to There's really no downside to having challengers at your events, especially if you're running free events It just increases the attendance. It increases the engagement. It makes them love your gym even more It's just it's a blast to have people that haven't been with the gym forever Attending these events. So give them event reminders. It's going to be a blast It's completely free. It comes with your membership or your challenge or whatever it may be So with all of these uh, be consistent with your event reminders and you'll have way better attendance So to follow that up, um, each announcement should always include directions on how to sign up So you have these five six different ways that you're making announcements and getting people Super pumped up about your events But you should always include how they can actually sign up for it Whether it's a sign-up sheet at the front desk, whether it's an online form Maybe you send them like a woofoo form or a google form or something that says hey If you want to go, uh, put your information in here and I'll get you signed up. Um, If it's an acuity scheduler, maybe the event requires them to do like a Quick meeting where you're going to introduce them supplements or something like that. Um, whether it's a text message You say hey, just text me back. However, you want to get them signed up and registered for the event Um, make sure that you tell them how to do that Give them the directions on how they can sign up. Don't just be like, yeah, you should come to this event be like come to this event and Uh, I'll sign you up Just tell me if you're gonna come or not and then you actually sign them up or if your trainers are making announcements before and after class Say hey, stop at the front desk and make sure you sign your name Um, and whatever the person you're bringing uh at the front desk on the sign-up sheet or hey, uh, If you post on facebook be like, hey, here's a form anybody that wants to come to the event Click this link and you can fill out this form with your name and what time you'll be there and who you're bringing And that way we can make sure that you're registered as soon as you submit it You are registered for our event. However, you decide to do it Um, just make sure that you have a really great way to collect all the names and all of the plus ones and everything So you can be prepared but also so you can start giving them appointment reminders you want to give appointment reminders Which are like a 48 hour reminder or a two hour friendly reminder. Okay. It really helps So give directions or instructions, uh, if needed so like directions to the place or whatever you want to you want to nurture your members with reminders because it will absolutely increase the attendance So don't forget to do that. Um, but you need to capture all of their information and who's actually planning on being there So with every announcement, um, make sure you let them know exactly how they can reserve their spot Um, and the information that they need to give you to reserve that spot and then you'll use it to send out these reminders So, um, this can be done, uh, an acuity or automatically or you can do it by hand However, you want to do it but send out a 48 hour and a two hour reminder before the events So, um, finally we'll get into fulfillment All right, so don't don't forget about having excellent fulfillment on events It will make it so much easier to pack the house next time. Okay So do a great job with actually pulling off an amazing event members will talk and word travels. All right So, um, they will tell all the other members if it was an amazing experience. They always do right? So make it an amazing experience and they'll talk to the to the challengers to the long time members Like man, have you bet were you at that event? Man? That was so much fun. I got a great burn It was it was a perfect way to start my weekend. It was just really really cool. You gotta make the next one They definitely do that. So really pull off and execute the event to the best of your ability Now take a ton of pictures including a group pic At every event tons of pictures Huge group of everybody together This is great for both marketing and then hanging up at the gym, right? You want to showcase your culture You want to showcase how strong your culture is? You can't get all of these people together to do things outside of a workout if you don't have a great culture Which means you do which means you want to showcase that whether you use it for facebook ads Or you post it into the members group to give a little bit of fomo fear of visiting out to anybody that wasn't at the event Whatever it may be You definitely want to showcase it, right? And then you want to print off like eight and a half by 11 or eight by 10 pictures And hang those all over your gym of these amazing events Because people see that when new challengers are coming in and they're touring the gym When members are thinking about leaving all of this stuff if they see all of these really cool member events They're going to feel like this is something really cool in a culture that they don't want to miss out on So print those off and get them get them hung up at your gym And in showcase your culture showcase how strong it is That's why you want to take as many pictures during the events as you possibly can So that's all I wanted to talk about. Um, these are just a few images of the events that we do And it's a lot of fun and we showcase it and we we do all the events that we talked about in this Presentation so put some time put some thought put some energy into it after watching this I would sit down and and I would plan out a year's worth of events What they look like what you'd like to do when you want to do fundraisers What causes you want to do when you want to do runs how those would be if you want to do hikes Where would you go? I would plan out all the details and once you have a 12 month game plan Completely set. That's when you want to announce it to your gym. Hey guys, we have a brand new initiative We're doing a year of events every month. We're going to have a new event. This is what it's going to be It's going to be a blast. Here's how you sign up And give them as many details as you possibly can get your team bought in Make sure you let them know like they have to come to events, but don't worry. They're getting paid But it'll be a blast And I would create that year-long game plan for events and then follow up on every single one Don't cancel an event Even if you have to change the venue or alternate it or even just sub it with a bootcamp Don't cancel it. Um fulfill on your events and if you do this, this will absolutely help you Increase your retention decrease your attrition people will have a lot of fun and you're going to have a really strong culture So don't underestimate the power of events And I hope you guys are able to go out and execute this make a 12 month plan and absolutely crush it
